session_summary: This session derived the W2 LRT-differential claims D1-D3 (H0 bias = tr(RIV_perp) in the I_com metric) through the full multimodal protocol (analytic-first, blind GPT-5.5 cross-model audit, simulation last), reconciled W1/H1, and landed the new @sec-lrt material in derivation.qmd. Marcus set the scope frame (W3 ladder parked as sequel) and the staged verification protocol up front, then ran an independent same-lineage audit of the derivation guide to close the one unchecked link (his own grading of the cross-model transcripts). Late in the session he pivoted toward the next session, contributing equipercentile/all-moment-matching domain judgment and driving the Kolen-Brennan literature acquisition pipeline.

coded_turns.quote:
SCOPE SPLIT is decided — this paper =
  bias correction + LRT (W1/W2); the W3 distributional-calibration ladder is a SEQUEL
coded_turns.note: Kickoff: locks scope split, enumerates LOCKED items, mandates analytic-first/blind-audit/sim-last protocol and D1-D3 work.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/024 D1-D3; D-06 scope split; @sec-lrt in derivation.qmd

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Launch GPT-5.5 xhigh cross-model audit (backgrounded, up to 24h)" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: Auto system notification that the backgrounded cross-model audit finished; triggers AI to grade the transcripts.

coded_turns.quote: I'm going to do an audit in an indpendent session of the quarto derviation guide. Give me the prompt
coded_turns.note: Imposes an independent same-lineage audit of the derivation as a verification gate; asks AI to author the audit prompt.

coded_turns.quote: Looks like it passes the audit. Is it worth it to address these parsing issues
coded_turns.note: Pastes full independent audit (PASS 0/0, re-derivation + grading-honesty check) and asks whether the cosmetic parsing INFOs warrant a fix.

coded_turns.quote: Great. So what's the next prompt? do we have a handoff document to the next session?
coded_turns.note: Approves the audit outcome and pivots to producing the next-session handoff document.

coded_turns.quote: What's the goal of the next session?
coded_turns.note: Organizing question that orients the planning of the next session's deliverable.

coded_turns.quote: Did we every analytically justify in the derivations guide the satorra bentler type correction and equipercentile?
coded_turns.note: Probes whether the SB-type correction and equipercentile equating were ever actually justified analytically, not just asserted.

coded_turns.quote: we want o do equipercentile equating here contrast first and second moment with "all moment matching" with the reference distribution so that the distribution of differences in AIC or BIC between any two models matches on all moments
coded_turns.note: Supplies the equating/all-moment-matching framing for the AIC/BIC difference distribution; offers to fetch context from a prior conversation.

coded_turns.quote: Can you look up equipercentile or equating in my .claude conversations?
coded_turns.note: Asks AI to search prior .claude conversations to recover the lost equipercentile discussion as a resource.

coded_turns.quote: ok pdf added for Kolen book. Please migrate to google drive
coded_turns.note: Adds the Kolen-Brennan equating PDF and instructs the AI to run the migrate step of the litrev pipeline.

coded_turns.quote: it's been indexed new BBT created. Go aheand and run lit-sync tool
coded_turns.note: Confirms indexing and new BBT export, then authorizes lit-sync to write the Kolen literature markdown.

ai_side.evidence: the handoff explicitly permits that — the next session just has to record the decision in todo/026 rather than make it silently
ai_side.note: AI surfaces the @sec-ic-vs-mi-spectral.qmd ordering as a decision for Marcus rather than choosing silently.

ai_side.evidence: the derivation shows the committed W2 `bias_correction` (the naive difference) is an approximation that's immaterial in the W2 design (~5%) but can be 3×+ off elsewhere
ai_side.note: AI proactively flags that the committed naive bias_correction can be 3x+ off in unfavorable designs.

ai_side.evidence: the copula point recorded in todo/023 says all-moment matching *for every pair simultaneously* via per-model transforms is impossible in general, so the claim will need the anchoring convention
ai_side.note: AI flags that the all-moment-matching headline is not attainable as phrased without an anchoring convention or per-pair scoping.

ai_side.evidence: the submission must not describe C5 as anything more than an empirical comparison arm — that wording caution is now baked into todo/026 so the drafting session can't drift into over-claiming it
ai_side.note: AI flags an over-claim risk on the committed C5 arm and bakes the wording caution into the handoff.

ai_side.evidence: the SB arm's disposition may flip from "retire" to "fix as rung 2 in-paper"
ai_side.note: AI surfaces that Marcus's equating answer could materially flip the SB arm disposition and todo/026.
